body.title: Preparse router — chunk + capacity → parallel distributed dispatch
body.result: preparse_router module: Batch/DispatchResult/RouterResult dataclasses + group_chunks + dispatch_batches + merge_csbs + route_preparse. Composes chunker + capacity-profile + local_preparse. Parallel ThreadPoolExecutor dispatch preserves input order. Fallback chain: per-batch fail → whole-input local → None-merged. 21 tests. Commit 44a8dcef. main.py integration deferred to follow-on once observed data shows where it helps.
body.description:
Preparse currently runs as a single call to one consumer (graph-tree OR local LLM OR, historically, web LLM — though T-pipeline-arch removed the web-LLM preparse path entirely). With atomic chunker (T-input-chunker) + capacity profile (T-cluster-router-capacity-profile) in place, build the dispatcher that consumes atomic chunks + the capacity/availability map, groups atoms into per-machine batches sized to each machine's safe ceiling, and fires them in parallel via the existing inference_gateway. Result-merge step reconciles cross-chunk references and dedupes memory queries.

**Affected files:** new `wild_igor/igor/cognition/preparse_router.py`; wire-in at the preparse callsite (likely `wild_igor/igor/main.py` or a cognition entrypoint — discovery step early in sprint, verify exact file); new `tests/test_preparse_router.py`. The main.py callsite touch is HIGH-inertia — PRE-APPROVAL REQUIRED from Akien before sprint begins. (Pattern: previous HIGH-inertia main.py touches in D-slow-query-triage and D-pursuit-layer were pre-approved inline; same procedure here.)
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (module docstring + class docstring); oop-first (PreparseRouter class with dispatch()/merge() methods); test-plan-or-why-not (covered); no-sqlite (no storage); no-new-memory-schemas (no memory writes from router).
**Scope boundary:** IN: consume atomic chunks + capacity map; group atoms into machine-sized batches (respecting ceilings); parallel dispatch via inference_gateway/cluster_router.route(); per-chunk result collection; merge/reconcile step (dedupe memory queries, resolve pronouns via context-carry); fallback chain (split smaller → local mini-LLM → graph-tree-only with low-confidence flag); record per-dispatch outcome back into cluster_router.capacity_profile.record(). OUT: the chunker itself; the capacity profiler itself; the actual preparse consumers (graph-tree, local LLM — those are their own modules); shadow-stream training corpus (separate architectural piece).
**Test plan:** atoms group respecting each machine's safe_ceiling; unavailable machines skipped (via is_overloaded + cluster_router health); all-unavailable triggers fallback (smaller groups, then local mini, then graph-tree-only); parallel dispatch verified (concurrent.futures or similar); results preserved in chunk order after merge; reconcile resolves pronouns using context-carry; outcomes feed back to capacity profiler; HIGH-inertia main.py integration test exercises end-to-end preparse path with mocked machines.
